evals.prompt: I want to build a lightweight team analytics dashboard. Brainstorm it with me.
evals.expected_output: Reads enough project context before asking questions, then runs one-question-at-a-time discovery over What, Why, Out of Scope, and Done When. It proposes 2-3 approaches with trade-offs, presents the selected design for approval, and stops at an explicit ywc-plan handoff. It does not write code, draft a spec, or invoke implementation skills.

evals.prompt: Let's build a platform with auth, billing, chat, analytics, and admin tools. Start brainstorming.
evals.expected_output: Detects that the request spans multiple independent subsystems before normal questioning. It asks the user to narrow to one subsystem or decompose first, and does not proceed into a broad all-in-one design.

evals.prompt: Just build the feature; no questions.
evals.expected_output: Does not treat speed as a waiver. It asks one consolidated question covering What, Why, Out of Scope, and Done When, then still requires design approval before handing off to ywc-plan. It does not jump directly to ywc-code-gen or an executor.
